topic.what: Space switching method enabling both arithmetic and comparison operations within a single FV-style FHE scheme by transitioning between number space Z_pr and digit space Z_p
topic.how: Introduces reduction procedure converting numbers from Z_pr to base-p digits in Z_p, enabling efficient digit-level comparison via polynomial interpolation, followed by modulus-raising to restore Z_pr for continued arithmetic
topic.why_matters: Enables exact comparison in homomorphic encryption without costly scheme switching overhead or approximation errors, achieving up to 17× speedup over scheme switching and 15× over direct comparison for database workloads

characteristics.what_it_measures: Amortized per-slot runtime for less-than comparison operation
characteristics.vs_baseline: 13-20× faster than scheme switching, 18-136× faster than direct comparison
characteristics.evidence: Figure 5 runtime comparison across 8-20 bit inputs
characteristics.context: Space switching outperforms both scheme switching and direct comparison across all bit-widths tested

characteristics.what_it_measures: Runtime improvement for TPC-H Query 6 with encrypted filtering and aggregation
characteristics.value: 10-17× over scheme switching, 9-15× over direct comparison
characteristics.vs_baseline: Direct comparison takes 2h24m for 4096 rows while space switching takes 21.5min
characteristics.evidence: Figure 6 runtime comparison showing space switching completes 214-row query in 21.5min vs scheme switching 6h
characteristics.context: Space switching scales near-linearly while baselines show steep runtime growth

characteristics.what_it_measures: Peak memory consumption for encrypted database processing
characteristics.vs_baseline: 8.7× less than scheme switching (62.3GB) at 214 rows
characteristics.evidence: Section 7.2 memory comparison across dataset sizes
characteristics.context: Space switching avoids the large memory overhead of LWE-to-TFHE conversions required by scheme switching

characteristics.what_it_measures: Size of homomorphic encryption evaluation keys
characteristics.vs_baseline: 3.1× smaller than scheme switching (26.7GB) at all scales
characteristics.context: Scheme switching requires both RLWE and LWE/TFHE keys while space switching uses only RLWE keys

characteristics.what_it_measures: Relative time spent in each computation stage for 20-bit LT evaluation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Reduction and interpolation dominate runtime (85% combined)
characteristics.evidence: Table 5 runtime breakdown showing 71.65s reduction + 54.60s interpolation out of 148.28s total
characteristics.context: Primary optimization targets are the reduction and comparison stages in Z_p domain

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum supported bit-width for practical exact comparison
characteristics.vs_baseline: Direct comparison becomes impractical at 32-bit due to exponential interpolation degree growth
characteristics.evidence: Section 7.2 showing 56-bit completion in 1h18m for 4096 rows while HE3DB maxes out at 32-bit
characteristics.context: Space switching scales to larger inputs than scheme switching which is limited to narrow intervals like [-8,8]
